data.epi_content:
When I opened the front door, Evan was standing there blankly.

Strangely, he didn't give off his usual impression, but rather something unique that was hard to describe...

The closest comparison would be the aura of that old man I saw at the execution grounds, someone who had likely cut down more people than anyone else.

"Ah, it's been a while, Evan. Come in. I was just having some tea."

What was unexpected was that he didn't throw a childish tantrum about coming over just to drink tea, but simply nodded and entered the room.

I felt slightly uneasy, like a country dog might feel when facing a dog catcher.

"Sit on that chair. What kind of tea would you like me to bring you?"

He answered with a slightly furrowed expression.

I'm not sure what exactly was making him uncomfortable.

"Whatever you recommend."

I put the tea leaves the Crown Prince had recommended to me into a cup and poured hot water over them.

Once again, the aroma in the room began to change.

Evan stared at the steam rising from the teacup for quite some time before looking at me.

With slightly trembling eyes, he wiped away a tear that had escaped from his right eye with his sleeve.

Then, as if nothing had happened, he maintained an expressionless face.

"I should be grateful that you're giving me your time when you'd normally be following Vivian around or studying magic."

I deliberately throw out a sarcastic comment.

"So why did you come here?"

At that, Evan takes a sip of the tea I handed him, raises an eyebrow slightly, and answers in a slick tone.

"I came to drink the tea you brewed, of course."

"No, that's not it. You must have heard about what happened between Vivian and me today."

He stops his fidgeting eyebrows and puts on a stoic expression again.

It gave me a slightly eerie feeling, like watching a robot with partially paralyzed facial muscles.

"I heard."

"Then if I had to name one reason why you came..."

As I continued speaking, Evan cut me off and answered.

As if there was no need to listen to useless talk.

"I came to be angry at you."

"And now?"

"Not at all. Looking at you doesn't give me any intense emotion... no, I don't feel angry or anything like that."

Evan speaks as if he no longer has such intentions.

Something has changed; Evan doesn't normally act like this.

"Originally you came here to be angry, right?

How dare a fallen noble—oh, maybe you don't know about that yet.

Anyway, a useless, talentless noble brat dared to mess with the great Vivian.

You needed to punish me and score points with Vivian!"

I mocked him by imitating his tone for the last part.

But truly, it's as if his emotional center was replaced with a computer while he was sleeping—he's not getting angry at all.

"I think that's how I felt at first."

"Really? Then what are you doing? You should be yelling at me and getting angry.

You should be putting me down and praising Vivian. Then, even if Vivian doesn't like it, she'll think you're on her side, won't she?"

So I continue with my sarcastic remarks and sneers.
data.epi_content2:
Trying to recreate the same picture as before, trying to create the future I know.

I don't know why, but I felt like I had to do this.

"That's what I thought when I came.

But why is it that when I see your face, suddenly I can't think of anything?

Just an incredibly distant yet nostalgic feeling. How should I put it?"

Evan looks at me while seeing something else.

But who could possibly overlap with me in his mind?

In Evan's mind, I'm still just a hothouse flower who has never experienced hardship, discomfort, poverty, or misfortune—a lady who enjoys brewing tea with broken flowers and placing them on top.

"Why, have you finally realized that Vivian deserved what she got?"

"I just don't know. Until just now, I was pacing back and forth in front of this door, furious.

Then suddenly, I started feeling sorry for you and sad... it's not normal."

"You're right, you're not normal."

"Then what could have changed the situation like this?"

As he said this, Evan suddenly stared at me intently and grabbed my shoulders tightly.

It hurt terribly, enough to make a cracking sound.

"...It hurts, Evan. Let go."

What could he possibly have experienced to change so suddenly?

His eyes were red, and despite my plea to let go, he shook his head and kept holding me tightly.

"I said it hurts. Let go."

I couldn't think of anything I might have changed in that short time. Could there be someone else who traveled back in time besides me?

But even so, Evan's emotions shouldn't change so drastically like this.

"I said it hurts!"

When I shouted, Evan finally seemed to come to his senses and released his grip on me.

"Ah, ah. I'm sorry."

"So suddenly, why are you acting like this?

Evan, you're supposed to remain someone who hates me, dislikes me, and finds me annoying until the end."

"I don't know."

"We're supposed to go beyond personal attacks to tearing down each other's families, freely hurling insults about relatives and kin.

Suddenly, what's changing you like this? Why won't you remain as someone I hate and dislike?"

"Until just now, when I was standing in front of this door, I was angry about you, then suddenly it changed to sadness.

Like I'm suffering from some mental illness. But now seeing you, I strangely feel extremely reassured, and a self-conviction that I was right is welling up."

"What the hell are you talking about?!"

In a moment of anger, I threw the teacup at Evan.

It didn't break.

Evan just got soaked with tea.

You're not Evan.

If you were Evan, you would yell at me, and at worst, push me.

You're cruelly indecisive toward others, petty, and selfish.

Until you fell for Vivian, I wasn't "others" to you, but once I became "others," you treated me so harshly.

"Suddenly, why now, why only at this moment are you changing?

Why do you care about me only now?! What has changed?!

Nothing has! Nothing! My family has fallen and my personality is still twisted!"

As I keep shouting, I feel like the blood isn't reaching my head.
data.epi_content3:
At the same time, my vision starts to blur and I begin to feel dizzy.

This damn Evan approaches me, trying to comfort me by patting me.

I don't entirely hate his touch.

But at the same time, I feel an insane sense of incongruity and push his hand away.

"All I can say is that I don't know either."

"Really? That's amazing.

Some people live reciting misfortune and curses because nothing changes or improves for them, while others can change immediately with just a little trigger, even when time returns to normal.

I'm jealous, so jealous. How wonderful it would be if I could do that too."

"Would it really be? Strangely, my mind feels like it's gone crazy.

When I close my eyes, I see myself killing ordinary villagers, and a heavily bearded version of myself offering treasures while muttering your name.

I don't seem much older though."

"Evan, maybe you should leave for today."

"If that's what you want."

"Then leave. It's too painful."

No matter how much I struggle, I can't change my fate or the future I'm going to face.

But he can completely change his mindset and way of life even when the same events happen at the same time.

Is that why they call him a protagonist?

I'm not sure if it's funny or regrettable.

Maybe he's the male protagonist.

Otherwise, what's the use of that brilliant mind of his?

He looked around my room and cast a spell.

Simultaneously, black magicians who had been deeply hidden throughout the floor emerged and reached out to Evan.

It was a terrifying sight, but what was that just now?

"Pick up all the broken glasses and water bottles below and throw them outside or clean them up..."

"Ah, if the glass below is too much trouble, I'll just clean it up myself later."

If I just put a bullet or two in my head, I could quickly escape from labor and everything else to find freedom.

They say there's no paradise where you escape to, but I could at least get a little bit of rest.

"No, Erica, I'm sorry for coming over today for no reason.

I'll visit often from now on..."

Hearing him say he'll visit often, my mind went blank for a moment.

Is he declaring that he'll come over to torment me now?

He would say he had no such intention, and judging by his current attitude, he really doesn't seem like he would torment me, but I was anxious.

I was always anxious and uneasy, a person who could prepare to escape at any time.

Because it's scary, you know.

I remember the misery of my first death, foolishly trying to die in the bathtub and dying very slowly, feeling death approach.

The second time, thinking the reason I couldn't die was because it was suicide, I went to the execution grounds to be killed by others, trying to disappear as dew on the execution field to escape the terrible mob violence, but it seems they left my corpse behind—my fault, I suppose.

The third time was just the best example of crazy people gathering together, repeating uncomfortable and wrong choices until finally meeting the worst end.

Well, after all, we left each other with wounds and something terrible before meeting our end.

Just because I was being bullied by Lydia, I wasn't going to do such things.

I had even promised myself to endure it to the end.

Well, they say the person who enters the bathroom and the person who comes out are different.

It might seem like a dirty example, but I just wanted to say that people are ultimately just a part of nature.

"Anyway, please don't visit often.

I'm afraid of you, you know."
